title: Cynorkis nervilabris
taxonomy: Cynorkis nervilabris is classified within the plant family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family comprises over 30,000 known species, making it the second-largest family of flowering plants. The Orchidaceae family is further divided into 763 genera, with Cynorkis being one of them. Cynorkis nervilabris is a species within the Cynorkis genus, which consists of approximately 30 species found primarily in tropical Africa. Cynorkis nervilabris is a type of terrestrial orchid, typically growing in shaded areas with high humidity.
uses: Cynorkis nervilabris is a species of orchid, and unfortunately, I don't have information on its medicinal uses.
benefits: Cynorkis nervilabris is a species of orchid, and as with many plants in the orchid family, it has been used in traditional medicine in various cultures. constituents: Cynorkis nervilabris contains various compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids.
